project.name: tagme-platform
project.description: TagMe is a Next.js hospitality platform for NFC guest experiences, hotel staff operations, TagMetrics analytics, and AVEX AI, backed by InsForge/Postgres. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand TagMe as an NFC/IoT hospitality platform backed by InsForge. It explains setup, database migration, seed data, Vercel deployment, and the pilot flow for Hotel Caribe.

tour.title: Application Shell
tour.description: The App Router root files establish the public Next.js application and shared layout behavior. These files orient how guest, staff, admin, executive, and supervisor route groups hang together.

tour.title: Guest NFC Flow
tour.description: The guest route group handles NFC tag entry and capture experiences. It is the user-facing path from a physical tag scan into destination selection, feedback, incidents, and room-aware capture.

tour.title: Operational Dashboards
tour.description: Admin, executive, staff, and supervisor pages organize the hotel operating workflows. Their paired components render metrics, scorecards, incidents, organization management, and content controls.

tour.title: API Routes
tour.description: The `app/api` tree provides server endpoints for auth, events, capture, AVEX, metrics, scorecards, reception, executive, and supervisor operations. These handlers form the backend-for-frontend boundary for the UI layers.

tour.title: InsForge Access
tour.description: The InsForge modules centralize browser, server, SSR, and shared client construction. They are the integration points between Next.js code and the hosted Postgres-based backend.

tour.title: Domain Logic
tour.description: Shared library modules implement AVEX prompting, analytics, capture submission, stays, scorecards, executive reporting, supervisor operations, and validators. This layer is where most reusable business rules live.

tour.title: Database Model
tour.description: The migration directories define the Postgres schema, row-level security, metrics views, executive tables, and staff/scorecard data model. These files explain the durable backend shape that API routes and libraries rely on.

tour.title: Automation Scripts
tour.description: The scripts directory seeds Hotel Caribe data, demo incidents, pilot users, scorecards, and migration workflows. These scripts are important for local setup, pilots, and repeatable operational demos.

tour.title: Validation Suite
tour.description: The tests cover unit rules, integration behavior, contract scenarios, and Playwright user journeys. They document expected behavior for NFC flows, reception, executive dashboards, supervisor operations, and security boundaries.
